records.ety: [L. proscribere, proscriptum, to write before, to publish, proscribe; pro before + scribere to write. See Scribe. The sense of this word originated in the Roman practice of writing the names of persons doomed to death, and posting the list in public.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Proscribed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Proscribing.]

defs: To doom to destruction; to put out of the protection of law; to outlaw; to exile; as, Sylla and Marius proscribed each other's adherents.
quotes: Robert Vere, Earl of Oxford, . . . was banished the realm, and proscribed.

defs: To denounce and condemn; to interdict; to prohibit; as, the Puritans proscribed theaters.
quotes: The Arian doctrines were proscribed and anathematized in the famous Council of Nice.
